message:
fix(http): preserve prefetched bytes on 101 protocol upgrades

Previously, ClientIncomingBody::new() discarded already-buffered bytes
when the response had an empty body (ClientBodyKind::Empty). This caused
data loss on 101 Switching Protocols responses where upgraded-protocol
bytes (e.g., WebSocket frames) could arrive in the same TCP read as the
final HTTP response headers.

Changes:
- Always preserve read_buf in ClientIncomingBody regardless of body kind,
  ensuring protocol-upgrade bytes are not silently dropped
- Add into_inner_with_buffer() that returns both the transport and any
  prefetched bytes, marked #[must_use] to prevent accidental loss
- Refactor into_inner() to delegate to into_inner_with_buffer() for
  backward compatibility (callers who don't need the buffer)
- Add test: request_streaming_upgrade_preserves_prefetched_bytes validates
  that a 101 response with trailing WebSocket frame bytes preserves them
  through into_inner_with_buffer()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
